label: fiction
title: The Trusted Treasurer
charge: Fraud by a charity's volunteer treasurer, diverting its funds
elements: The accused directed it to himself.
He did so dishonestly, meaning to gain.

beats.text: Board members line up to vouch for the accused's decades of unpaid service. 'He would never take a cent,' one says.
beats.reveal_note: Reputation is disarming — and it is precisely what lets a trusted insider move money unwatched for years.

beats.text: Bank records show 23 transfers from the charity to an account whose only signatory is the accused, paid to a 'supplier' that does not exist.
beats.reveal_note: You do not accidentally invent a supplier you alone control and pay it 23 times. This is the spine of the case.

beats.text: The accused explains the discrepancies as hopeless bookkeeping: 'I'm a volunteer, not an accountant.'
beats.reveal_note: Incompetence is a tempting excuse, but it does not create a fake company registered to your own account.

beats.text: File metadata shows that the night after the auditor's first email, the accused edited the ledger, relabelling the transfers.
beats.reveal_note: Altering the records the moment questions arrive is the act of a man who knows what they will find.

beats.text: The judge instructs that dishonesty may be inferred from concealment and the deliberate alteration of records.
beats.reveal_note: It licenses the obvious inference: the midnight edit was not tidying up, it was hiding.

twist: The reputation and the 'I'm just bad with spreadsheets' defence are disarming — which is how it worked for years. But you don't accidentally invent a supplier you alone control, and you don't edit the ledger the night the auditor emails. The trust was the cover, not the alibi.
